---
title: "Home"
---
## Welcome!

This site provides an **educational and research-support resource** for curated *GATA2* variants, integrating evidence from public databases and the primary literature.


## Mabel McCurdy (Minji Jung)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="65%"}
Hello! I am a Ph.D.-trained scientist with a background in functional genomics, transcriptional regulation, and variant interpretation, with a research focus on GATA2-associated disease mechanisms.

This project grew out of my doctoral work investigating how pathogenic *GATA2* variants alter genome-wide regulatory programs, including enhancer dysregulation and inflammatory signaling. I created this resource to consolidate clinical and functional evidence on *GATA2* variants and to further develop my skills in computational curation and clinical genomics–adjacent workflows.

I am actively pursuing industry roles in **variant assessment and clinical genomics**, where accurate interpretation and clear communication of genetic evidence directly impact patient care.
:::

::: {.column width="35%"}
![](assets/images/mabel_image.jpg){fig-alt="Mabel McCurdy" width=260px style="border-radius: 16px;"}
:::
:::

## Connect

- **LinkedIn:**  
  http://www.linkedin.com/in/mabel-mccurdy-655136118

- **Curriculum Vitae (PDF):**  
  [Download CV](assets/Mabel_McCurdy_CV.pdf)


## Disclaimer
**This resource is for educational and research purposes only.**  
It is not intended for clinical diagnosis, treatment decisions, or patient management. Variant interpretations may evolve as new evidence becomes available. Because source materials occasionally contain inconsistencies or typographical errors, some entries may be incomplete or subject to revision. (Last updated Dec 2025.)
---
